items.title: New Trump tariffs 'make it harder for Australian businesses to compete'

children.content: The Business Council of Australia says a new tariff will make it harder for Australian businesses to compete in the US and could reduce investment.

items.title: Why did Trump slap a 12.5pc tariff on Australian imports?

children.content: The tariffs are supposed to combat forced labour, but one expert says the US president was just looking for another legal way to tax foreign imports.

image.altText: A US flag flies near a ship at a shipping port

items.title: Defence minister confirms former senior naval officer faces court martial

children.content: A former senior naval officer will face charges in a military trial set down for up to six weeks from September 14 with the military court ordering their identity remain suppressed.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaks at a press conference.

articles.title: The Commonwealth Games have never opened like this, but maybe they should

children.content: The 2026 Commonwealth Games are like no other that came before them and the opening ceremony was similarly unique in a way that will make it hard to go back.

image.altText: A silhouetted bag piper at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony.

articles.title: Swimmer praises Rinehart for bonuses amid warning to not thank billionaire

children.content: Swimming star Kyle Chalmers praises billionaire Gina Rinehart over bonuses for Commonwealth Games medals, as a report suggests athletes have been told not to thank the mining magnate.

image.altText: Australian swimmer Kyle Chalmers speaks to the media at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Comm Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

children.content: It's officially day one of the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, with the the first medals of the Games up for grabs in the para powerlifting. Follow live.

items.title: Broken lane rope halts Aussie charge at Comm Games pool

updates.title: Sisters competing against each other in the pool, for different countries
Winner Aaron Wilson on track to defend his title

items.title: Hundreds attend memorial for murdered mum Jana Armstrong

children.content: Jana Armstrong has been remembered as a beloved sister, loving mother and valued member of the Toowoomba community following her death two weeks ago.

image.altText: A sign with a woman's face and the words 'Jana Never Forgotten'.

items.title: Friends turned millionaires take tech company shares battle to court

children.content: The millionaire co-founder of an Australian video tech company behind some of Hollywood’s biggest blockbusters has told the Federal Court a decade of hostility by his fellow shareholders has unfairly barred him from selling his lucrative stake in the billion-dollar business.

image.altText: A group of men ride horses in a ranch.

items.title: Reginald Chard, Australia's last surviving Kokoda veteran, dies at 102

children.content: Australia's last surviving Kokoda campaign veteran, Reginald James Chard OAM, has died aged 102.

children.content: After an awful start against South Sydney the Storm have rebounded well to take the lead before halftime. Follow live.

items.title: Cats surge against Dees, Eagles up for derby fight against Dockers

children.content: Melbourne hosts Geelong at the MCG with one club trying to stay in the top six and the other trying to break in, before the Dockers and Eagles meet in the Western Derby. Follow live.

items.title: Four Corners withdraws episodes by sacked reporter

items.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding

children.content: Police say they stopped the 22-year-old in a BMW Coupe about 1am today and allege he was travelling 109 kilometres per hour on the Anzac Bridge.

items.title: Rare eye disorder linked to Ozempic-like medications

children.content: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has issued a safety warning for a rare, but serious risk of vision impairment from taking GLP-1-based drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy.

items.title: Bondi attack had traumatic effect on MP's son, royal commission hears

items.title: How and how often should you be cleaning out your freezer?

children.content: When it comes to kitchen cleaning, the freezer is the last frontier — a forgotten wasteland where food goes to die. But it's not hard to clean, and there are good reasons to do it.
